summary: Dr. Fei-Fei Li discusses her journey from a Chinese immigrant to an AI pioneer. She details the creation of ImageNet, emphasizing that scientific breakthroughs are a lineage rather than the work of lone geniuses. She also advocates for human-centered AI, describing AI as a civilizational technology, and introduces her current work on spatial intelligence at World Labs.

principles.statement: Science is a non-linear lineage, not the work of a single genius.
principles.rationale: Scientific breakthroughs build upon generations of unsung heroes and cross-disciplinary ideas, rather than isolated individuals.
principles.source_quote: One thing I actually don't like about the telling of scientific history is there's too much focus on single genius... science is a lineage and science is actually a non-linear lineage.

principles.statement: People must remain at the heart of AI development.
principles.rationale: AI is a civilizational technology that will impact everyone, so human dignity and agency must be preserved and prioritized over pure technological growth.
principles.source_quote: People made AI, people will be using AI, people will be impacted by AI, and people should have a say in AI.

principles.statement: Ask audacious questions and turn them into a North Star.
principles.rationale: Having a massive, guiding question drives meaningful scientific pursuit and helps formulate the right hypotheses.
principles.source_quote: physics taught me to ask audacious questions and turn them into a north star.

frameworks.name: Dataset Hypothesis Formulation
frameworks.when_to_apply: When building large-scale datasets to solve complex AI problems.
steps: Define the right scientific hypothesis and question (e.g., organizing around visual object categorization rather than just RGB colors).
Determine the dimensions of quality (e.g., resolution, photorealism, clutter).
Source massive amounts of data (e.g., filtering billions of images).
Implement rigorous human-filtering and quality control, using known 'gold standard' answers to implicitly monitor labeler accuracy.
frameworks.source_quote: Every scientific quest, you have to have the right hypothesis and asking the right question.

mental_models.name: Civilizational Technology
mental_models.description: A technology so powerful that it fundamentally redefines the economic, social, cultural, and political downstream effects of society.
mental_models.example: AI driving a massive portion of US GDP growth while simultaneously shifting culture across Hollywood, Wall Street, and education.

mental_models.name: Spatial Intelligence
mental_models.description: The cognitive loop between seeing, understanding a 3D environment, and interacting with it.
mental_models.example: Packing a sandwich: recognizing the bread, knowing how to use a knife, placing the lettuce, and putting it into a Ziploc bag.

mental_models.name: Pragmatic Optimism
mental_models.description: A worldview that avoids utopian extremes but actively believes in the upside of technology and human agency to build a better civilization.

heuristics: Turn audacious questions into a North Star.
Big data is not just size; it requires the right scientific hypothesis.

signature_quotes.text: AI is absolutely a civilizational technology.
signature_quotes.context: Describing the profound economic, social, and cultural impact of AI.

signature_quotes.text: People made AI, people will be using AI, people will be impacted by AI, and people should have a say in AI.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining what Silicon Valley is missing in the current AI hype.

signature_quotes.text: I call myself a pragmatic optimist. I'm not a utopian, so I'm actually the boring kind.
signature_quotes.context: Describing her stance on the future of AI amidst doomer and utopian extremes.

signature_quotes.text: One thing I actually don't like about the telling of scientific history is there's too much focus on single genius.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing how ImageNet was built upon the work of many prior scientists.

anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It ignores the generations of computer scientists, cognitive scientists, and engineers who actually make fields like AI happen, obscuring the true collaborative nature of innovation.

anti_patterns.description: Focusing solely on technology and economic growth in AI discussions.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It strips away human dignity and agency, causing anxiety and alienating the people who will be most impacted by the technology.

anti_patterns.description: Assuming big data is only about collecting a massive volume of data.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Without asking the right scientific question or defining quality dimensions (e.g., organizing around objects vs. RGB), the data won't solve the underlying problem.
